title: Smoky Nut Slovakian Stir-fry
description: A cozy smoky and juicy dish featuring nutmeg, cured meats, and Mediterranean-friendly flavors to create a comforting culinary experience.

ingredients.name: Lamb Chops
ingredients.notes: About the size of your palm

ingredients.name: Pistachios
ingredients.notes: Shelled and lightly toasted for garnish

ingredients.name: Minced Shallots
ingredients.notes: For sweetness and depth of flavor

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: To deglaze the pan if needed

ingredients.name: Nutmeg Powder
ingredients.notes: Freshly grated for a burst of flavor

instructions: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Pat dry the lamb chops with paper towels.
Sauté onions, minced shallots, and garlic in 1 tablespoon olive oil until softened. Season lightly with salt.
Add cayenne pepper and continue cooking for another minute until fragrant.
Deglaze pan with water, scraping up any browned bits on the bottom. Let it reduce slightly.
Transfer onions mixture to a baking dish. Top each lamb chop with 2 slices of cured ham. Sprinkle almonds evenly over them.
Bake in preheated oven for about 18-20 minutes or until internal temperature reaches 145°F (63°C) for medium-rare meat. Let rest for a few minutes before serving.
Drizzle finished lamb chops with extra virgin olive oil and garnish with shelled pistachios and freshly grated nutmeg.
Serve hot with a side of crusty bread or steamed vegetables.
